events.title: ASEAN envoy to engage all Myanmar stakeholders at Manila summit

events.title: Ukrainian gains should bring Putin to the negotiating table: Kubilius

events.title: 'Marine Le Pen is France's last hope', says Musk amid interference claims
events.summary: In a post on X on Wednesday, Elon Musk backed Marine Le Pen ahead of France’s presidential vote, prompting claims of foreign interference and electoral risk.

events.title: Diamonds hidden in shoes: Vietnam probe highlights India smuggling link

events.title: Thailand considers suspending nearly 6,000 officials over exam scandal

events.title: China says Papua New Guinea has closed Taiwan's office in the country

events.title: What inflation? South Koreans continue to queue for ginseng chicken soup in peak summer
events.summary: Many believe that eating the soup on the hottest days induces perspiration, cools the body and replenishes nutrients.

events.title: Decades of denial: Death certificate for disappeared reopens Kashmir wounds

events.title: EU Court of Justice clears way for Puigdemont amnesty as it backs Spain

events.title: VK Sells RuStore to Developer’s CEO Following EU Sanctions
events.summary: RuStore was created as a domestic Android alternative, stepping in after Apple and Google limited access for Russian users.

events.title: One person reportedly killed as Algeria battles wildfires

events.title: Japan notifies local governments they may ‘ban’ private lodgings amid problems

events.title: Czech citizen detained in China faces espionage investigation

events.title: Japan PM’s government approval rating below 50% for first time

events.title: Trump Administration Argues for Fencing Off Park Next to White House

events.title: Germany's Delivery Hero backs €13 billion takeover by Uber

events.title: How Israel Tried and Failed to Change the Iranian Regime
events.summary: The U.S. and Israel were united in going to war with Iran to change the regime. Mark Mazzetti, a Times investigative reporter, explains how the failure of Israel to install Mahmoud Ahmadinejad as Iran’s new leader led the U.S. to narrow its efforts in Iran to just launching attacks with no plan for a new government.

events.title: Canada: train crew escapes flames near Armstrong, Ontario
events.summary: A train crew was surrounded by wildfire flames near Armstrong, Ontario, Canada, on 13 July 2026. The Canadian National Railway crew escaped safely as more than 160 fires burned across the region.

events.title: "It's freedom": Border residents react to end of Gibraltar-Spain checks

events.title: Ukrainians hold rare wartime protest over defence minister’s dismissal
events.summary: For the first time since last summer’s protests in support of their country's anti‑corruption watchdogs, Ukrainians have mobilised online over the dismissal of Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov. A wave of social media posts quickly turned into calls to gather across Ukrainian cities on Thursday.

events.title: Greece pins hopes on heat-seeking satellites to predict wildfires

events.title: China is building early warning system to spot Earth-smashing asteroids

China planned to deploy multiple telescopes on the ground, alongside a constellation of satellites in orbit, state-run Science and Technology Daily quoted Li Mingtao, chief scientist at the asteroid monitoring and early-warning research centre under the China National Space Administration, as saying. None of the asteroids that had been discovered...

events.title: After KFC, cyberattack hits Japanese ice cream giant Glico

events.title: Iran warns Strait of Hormuz is a 'red line' and will resist until the end

events.title: Toronto choked by wildfire smoke, records world's worst air quality

events.title: MEP: German military law makes citizens 'think about contribution'

events.title: Volcanic eruption is on going for Mayon in Philippines

events.title: Rights groups urge Thailand not to deport Chinese journalist to China

events.title: Fairytale destinations across Europe that look too magical to be real

events.title: Chat Control is 'treating everyone as a suspect', says expert

events.title: Seed by seed: Women of Tampenau nursing Sabah’s rainforests back to recovery
events.summary: In a small Sabah village, women are raising thousands of native saplings to restore one of the state's most degraded rainforests.

events.title: Starmer tells Ukraine UK support will 'endure' after his departure

events.title: What is the EU’s plan to triple energy storage as renewable power goes to waste?

events.title: French PM Lecornu arrives in Rabat for talks amid diplomatic reset

events.title: China’s lab monkey prices hit Covid-era highs on new drug R&D demand
events.summary: A boom in China’s new drug research and development is driving laboratory monkey prices back to coronavirus pandemic-era highs, forcing some researchers to halt their studies. According to Guo Xiangyu, a scientist at Jinan University in Guangzhou whose research focuses on developing non-human primate models for major brain diseases, laboratory monkey prices have risen steadily since Chinese New Year. Suppliers are now generally quoting about 180,000 yuan (US$27,000) per animal. “It is not just a...

events.title: Azerbaijan at 'real peace' with Armenia but wants it to change constitution

events.title: Food inflation is finally slowing. So why could your supermarket bill start rising again?
events.summary: Food inflation in Europe is easing, but the recent oil and fertiliser shock could still push up supermarket prices with a delay of more than a year, while this summer's extreme weather is likely to be a bigger driver of eurozone food inflation next year than the Iran war, according to economists.

events.title: Why this summer's heatwaves could raise your supermarket bill next year
